_README: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every crossing-site decision. Authored by hand; nothing else is.
build_blockers.py generates the blocker files, the joins file's decision fields and the export
description FROM THIS. test_decision_consistency.py fails if any artifact drifts from it.
It exists because on 2026-07-14 the same decision was written into five places by hand, they
disagreed within a day, and a client deliverable ended up asserting something false (D87 F7).
TWO INDEPENDENT AXES. Each is authored on its own; NEITHER is derived from the other.
crossing_exists  true | false | "unknown"   is there a real crossing here?
guest_routable   true | false | "unknown"   may a guest drive through it?
D89 claimed to split these and did not: both were still computed from one `status` field, so
the three legal combinations were the only ones expressible (confirmed=yes/yes, private=yes/no,
unconfirmed=no/no) and `load_manifest` REJECTED anything else. The case we actually had - S06,
'the crossing exists, we don't know if guests may use it' - was literally unrepresentable, so
it got rounded to 'confirmed' and the site went routable on an inference (D90 F2). Naming two
booleans is not separating them. These are now stored, not derived.
"unknown" is a first-class answer and the honest one far more often than not. It is NOT
false: `crossing_exists:"unknown"` says we have not established a crossing, not that we
established there is none.
ROUTING: a site is cut unless guest_routable is exactly true. Both false and "unknown" cut.
closure:
open    - cut pending an answer. If Solio answers, unblocking is the CORRECT response.
settled - routable on evidence, or closed by agreement. Does not move on 'confirmation'.
routable_basis - REQUIRED when guest_routable is true, forbidden otherwise:
'recorded-drive' - a GPX drive crossed the river here. CHECKED against the joins file's
evidence: claiming this without crossing_confirmed evidence FAILS the
build. It was a free-text label until someone set S06 to 'recorded-drive'
with zero recorded crossings and the whole suite stayed green (D90 F2).
'quote'          - Solio's words state DRIVABILITY.
'inference'      - we concluded it. Recording an inference does not make it evidence: it
is not a basis for routing a guest, only for asking a better question.
An inference is never laundered into a quote. If it is an inference, `inference` says why.
RULE (D79, and the one I broke within the hour - D87 F1, and again on S06 - D90 F2): naming a
place is NOT confirming you can drive through it. 'It's the Orphanage Road' identifies a road.
It does not say the river join we inferred there is a real crossing a guest may drive. Only set
guest_routable=true on a recorded drive crossing, or words that speak to DRIVABILITY, not
identity. Three review rounds found this same over-read, each time in a different disguise.
quote: Callan's own words, verbatim. If a field cannot be traced to a quote or to recorded
evidence, it is an inference - say so in `inference`, do not launder it into an axis.

_provenance.context: Reply to the 2026-07-09 road-data pack: 7 unconfirmed crossing sites of 22.
_provenance.also_said: Happy with the Solio Roads V2 File

S01.name: near Tharua Bridge — separate crossing S of the drawn bridge
inference: Proven by a recorded GPX drive crossing the river within 150 m of the join
(2026-07-09 regrade). Evidence, not words — never part of the Callan ask.

S05.name: Kingfisher Dam
quote: Yeah - you can drive to a sort of view point/ pick nic spot
I'd say the road is pretty accurate on the map
I believe it's an end point
inference: 'drive TO a viewpoint' + 'I believe it's an end point' reads as a destination, not a
through route - so we keep it cut. But 'I believe' is hedged, so this is NOT recorded as
settled fact (D87 F2 downgraded it from permanent/no-crossing back to unconfirmed).
RETRACTED: 'opening S05 costs zero routes' was never established. The experiment's extra
node-pair lands in a component that gets pruned, so the identical SHA proved nothing; a
plausible real connection moves 8/36 POI pairs. Do not repeat that claim.
If a real crossing is ever confirmed here, unblocking is the CORRECT response - which is
exactly why this is `unconfirmed` and not `private`.
CROSSING EXISTS: quoted. 'Crossing' answers the topology question directly - unlike S16/S21,
which name the ROAD the join sits on and say nothing about the join itself.
GUEST ROUTABLE: UNKNOWN. Nobody has asked. CUT until someone does.
The reasoning that kept it open for three review rounds: Callan flagged access restrictions
explicitly where he wanted them (S18/S20, unprompted), said nothing of the sort here, and
S06 is drawn on the guest poster - so the default should be that guests may use it. That is
a decent bet. It is not an answer, and D79 exists precisely to stop me routing on a decent
bet: 'Crossing' answers topology, not permission. Round 1 called it an over-read; round 2
called it an over-read; I answered round 2 by writing `routable_basis: inference` next to
`guest_routable: true` and calling it fixed. Labelling the guess did not stop the app acting
on the guess - the site stayed in the graph and the joins file still read 'guests may drive
it'. An inference is a reason to ask a better question, not a basis for routing (D90 F2).
COST OF BLOCKING: 15/36 POI pairs, including all three Kingfisher recoveries
(rhinogate->kingfisher 9.36->10.95 km, kingfisher->yellowthorn 6.18->7.33,
kingfisher->naribo 7.58->9.18). Nothing becomes unreachable - every POI still routes, just
further; worst case +1.60 km. S06 is the ONLY site whose answer buys anything: S16 and S21
are worth exactly zero. That cost is why this is worth an ask, and is not a reason to assume
the answer. Nav is off, so today it costs nothing real.
TO SETTLE: 'May guests drive through the crossing at S06?' One answer, one field.
Name NOT confirmed: our 'Browns Bridge' guess is dropped, he never said Browns.

S16.name: Mount Kenya River Road (part)
quote: S16 = Part of the Mount Kenya River Road
inference: Identifies a ROAD. Says nothing about whether the river join we inferred is a real,
guest-drivable crossing. Unblocked on 14 Jul in breach of our own rule; reblocked
2026-07-15 (D87 F1). Costs zero POI pairs, so there was never any reason to over-read it.
To promote: ask 'does the road actually cross the river here, and may guests drive through?'
Proven by a recorded GPX drive crossing the river within 150 m of the join
(2026-07-09 regrade). Evidence, not words — never part of the Callan ask.

S18.name: Marriotts Private Road
quote: S20 & S18 = Marriotts Private Road - May be a good idea to restrict access here
inference: Crossing is real; access is the decision. Callan proposed the restriction himself, so this
is agreed on both sides, not a suggestion folded into scope.
SCOPE: the two CROSSINGS are cut, not 'the drive' - we have no geometry for the Marriotts
road as a whole. Cutting them leaves it as three dead-end spurs, so through-driving is
structurally impossible. JW Marriott stays reachable: it is a lodge guests sleep in.
Proven by a recorded GPX drive crossing the river within 150 m of the join
(2026-07-09 regrade). Evidence, not words — never part of the Callan ask.

S20.name: Marriotts Private Road
quote: S20 & S18 = Marriotts Private Road - May be a good idea to restrict access here
inference: See S18. This is the crossing W of JW Marriott; the parked jw-bridge connector in
connectors.unconfirmed.geojson belongs here and must NOT be re-added for guest routing.

S21.name: Orphanage Road
inference: Identifies a ROAD. 'Looks good' is not topology approval of the inferred river join.
Same breach and same fix as S16 (D87 F1); reblocked 2026-07-15 at zero route cost.
Also killed our 'likely Waterbuck Bridge' guess - Waterbuck is now UNLOCATED.
Never answered. The orphanage corner, outside GPX coverage. Parked - not worth an ask.

_inventory: ALL 22 sites are listed. This is deliberate and load-bearing: a site that is merely
ABSENT from this file silently drops out of governance — nothing blocks it, nothing
checks it, and it stays routable. Round 2 proved it: deleting S06 from the manifest
left the whole suite green. Prose saying 'the rest are fine' is not an inventory (D89).
test_decision_consistency.py asserts every site in the joins file appears here.
